title: ficus tinctoria subsp. gibbosa
intro: Ficus tinctoria subsp. gibbosa, also known as the Indian laurel or jungle borage, is a plant species in the fig family (Moraceae) native to tropical regions of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ficus tinctoria subsp. gibbosa include its potential to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Ficus tinctoria subsp. gibbosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
